text:
There was once a mouse named Mousey who knew all the secrets of a mouse hole. One day, Mousey decided to invite some friends out for a picnic. They gathered wildflowers, cheese twists, and chocolate cookies. Everyone ate happily.

Afterwards, Mousey said, "You know, you're our best friend."

Your friends disagreed. "Who could love a mouse?" they giggled.

"But I have lots of little friends," Mousey said happily.

One tiny rabbit said, "Oh no! I wouldn't even hug you."

Mousey bounced up and hugged the tiny rabbit. They enjoyed each other's company. Suddenly, a fox swooped down. The rabbits ran away. Mousey waved goodbye.

"That was nice," said the same rabbit. "I think I'd like a friend like you."

Off they went. Every day they'd go out together. They shared stories about their adventures. They climbed trees together. They even learned to play ball!

Years went by.
